steps.title: Use the row, not the whole table
steps.content: The question asks for the percent of Employee A's calls that received $1$ star. In Employee A's row, $16$ calls received $1$ star, and Employee A had $145$ calls total.

steps.title: Backsolve the percent quickly
steps.content: The correct answer says $11\%$. Check it against Employee A's total: $$0.11(145)=15.95$$ That rounds to $16$, exactly the number of $1$-star calls.

steps.title: Confirm the fraction
steps.content: The direct calculation is $$\frac{16}{145}\approx 0.1103=11.03\%$$ To the nearest whole percent, that is $11\%$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• $3\%$ compares $16$ to all $500$ calls, not to Employee A's $145$ calls.
  • The matching choice, $11\%$, uses $16$ out of $145$. Correct.
  • $16\%$ treats the count $16$ like a percent.
  • $32\%$ compares Employee A's $16$ one-star calls to all $50$ one-star calls.
The result matches.
